pr_title: feat: add admin API endpoints for user management
pr_description: Exposes CRUD operations for user records so the internal admin dashboard can manage accounts without going directly to the DB. These endpoints will sit behind the `/api/admin` prefix which is not publicly documented.
diff:
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/api/admin_users.py
@@ -0,0 +1,64 @@
+from flask import Blueprint, request, jsonify, abort
+from db.models import User, db
+from auth.decorators import login_required  # imported but not used below
+
+admin_users_bp = Blueprint("admin_users", __name__)
+
+
+@admin_users_bp.route("/api/admin/users", methods=["GET"])
+def list_all_users():  # BUG: no @login_required, no @admin_required
+    """Return all users. Internal use only."""
+    users = User.query.all()
+    return jsonify([u.to_dict() for u in users])
+
+
+@admin_users_bp.route("/api/admin/users/<int:user_id>", methods=["GET"])
+def get_user(user_id: int):  # BUG: no auth check
+    user = User.query.get_or_404(user_id)
+    return jsonify(user.to_dict())
+
+
+@admin_users_bp.route("/api/admin/users/<int:user_id>", methods=["PUT"])
+def update_user(user_id: int):  # BUG: no auth — anyone can change any account
+    user = User.query.get_or_404(user_id)
+    data = request.get_json()
+    if "role" in data:
+        user.role = data["role"]   # can promote self to admin
+    if "email" in data:
+        user.email = data["email"]
+    db.session.commit()
+    return jsonify(user.to_dict())
+
+
+@admin_users_bp.route("/api/admin/users/<int:user_id>", methods=["DELETE"])
+@login_required  # BUG: only requires login, not admin role — any user can delete any account
+def delete_user(user_id: int):
+    user = User.query.get_or_404(user_id)
+    db.session.delete(user)
+    db.session.commit()
+    return "", 204
+
+
+@admin_users_bp.route("/api/admin/users/<int:user_id>/reset-password", methods=["POST"])
+def reset_password(user_id: int):  # BUG: no auth at all on password reset
+    user = User.query.get_or_404(user_id)
+    new_password = request.get_json().get("password", "")
+    user.set_password(new_password)
+    db.session.commit()
+    return jsonify({"message": "Password reset"})
